prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Spanish  
El mejor momento para sembrar un sueño fue ayer; el siguiente mejor momento es este instante.  
Note: Keeps the urgency while swapping “tree” for “dream,” a culturally resonant symbol of long-term hope.

Japanese  
樹を植えるなら、タイムマシンがない今の手元のスコップを動かすことだ。  
Note: Re-roots the proverb in a very Japanese acceptance of irretrievable time, stressing present action over regret.
